content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The issue is clear: PostHog is configured with `host: 'https://code-insights.app'` (line 12), meaning it tries to send events to `https://code-insights.app/batch`. But your Vercel site doesn't have a `/batch` endpoint — it's a Next.js marketing site that returns 404/405 for unknown routes.

This is a **PostHog reverse-proxy pattern** — the idea is to proxy PostHog API calls through your own domain to avoid ad-blockers. But the proxy route was never set up on the Vercel side.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The `POSTHOG_HOST` is set to `https://code-insights.app` but your Vercel site has no proxy route to forward `/batch` requests to PostHog's actual API (`https://us.i.posthog.com` or `https://eu.i.posthog.com`).

Two options to fix this:

**Option A: Add a Vercel rewrite** in the web repo to proxy `/batch` → PostHog's ingest API. This keeps the ad-blocker bypass working.

**Option B: Point directly to PostHog's API** — change `POSTHOG_HOST` to `https://us.i.posthog.com` (or `eu.i.posthog.com`). Simpler, but ad-blockers can block it.

Since this is a CLI tool (not a browser), ad-blockers aren't a concern. **Option B is the simplest fix.** Want me to go with that, or do you prefer setting up the Vercel proxy?

message.content: Option B for cli telemetry. Retain the code-insights.app for dashboard telemetry events as it may have adblocker in web browsers. makes sense?

content.text: That makes perfect sense — CLI runs in Node.js (no ad-blockers), dashboard runs in a browser (ad-blockers possible). Let me check the dashboard telemetry to understand the full picture.

Good — the setup is clean. Here's the plan:

**Change:** In `cli/src/utils/telemetry.ts`, update `POSTHOG_HOST` to `https://us.i.posthog.com` (PostHog's direct ingest endpoint). Dashboard telemetry stays as-is with `code-insights.app`.

**But** — the dashboard proxy also doesn't work yet (same 405 error would occur from the browser). The Vercel rewrite for `/batch` → PostHog needs to be added in the web repo. Want me to handle that separately, or is the Vercel proxy already configured?

Let me make the CLI fix now.

content.content: The file /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/cli/src/utils/telemetry.ts has been updated successfully.

lines: // PostHog write-only API key (public — this is the standard PostHog pattern;
// write-only keys can only ingest events, not read data).
// Touch file path that tracks whether the disclosure has been shown.
// Content is the CLI version — if version doesn't match current, notice is re-shown.

content.text: Done. Now rebuild to pick up the change:

content.text:
Build passes. Try your sync again:

```bash
node dist/index.js sync
```

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **CLI → `us.i.posthog.com`** (direct ingest): No ad-blockers in Node.js, so the proxy pattern adds complexity with no benefit. PostHog's `us.i.posthog.com` is their US ingest endpoint — use `eu.i.posthog.com` if you ever need EU data residency.
- **Dashboard → `code-insights.app`** (reverse proxy): Browser-based telemetry benefits from proxying through your own domain because ad-blockers maintain blocklists of known analytics domains like `posthog.com`. However, **the Vercel proxy route (`/batch` → PostHog) still needs to be configured** in the web repo, or dashboard telemetry will hit the same 405 error silently.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The dashboard telemetry will fail silently in the browser right now too (same missing `/batch` route). Want me to add the Vercel rewrite in the web repo to complete the proxy setup?

file.content:
---
title: "What Your AI Coding Sessions Reveal About Your Development Patterns"
description: "Developers run hundreds of AI coding sessions but rarely look back at them. Session data reveals surprising patterns about how you actually work — and where you lose time."
date: "2026-03-07"
author: "Srikanth Rao"
tags: ["ai-coding", "developer-patterns", "productivity"]
---

Most developers can tell you what they worked on today. Fewer can tell you *how* they worked on it — how many turns a task actually took, whether they explored more than they edited, or how often a "quick fix" quietly consumed an hour.

That's not a character flaw. It's a tooling gap. AI coding sessions generate a surprising amount of structured data — message counts, tool calls, file touches, timestamps — but most AI coding tools don't surface session-level analytics anywhere in their UI. The terminal closes, and the data sits unused in log files.

When you do look at it, patterns emerge that intuition alone doesn't surface.

## The "quick task" that takes 45 minutes

This is the most common pattern, and the most humbling.

You open a session intending to make a small change. Rename a variable, fix a type error, update a config value. Ten minutes, tops. But the session data tells a different story: 30 messages, a dozen file reads, edits across multiple files, and a duration that quietly stretched past half an hour.

content.text:
Good. Now I'll add the PostHog rewrites to `vercel.json` and create a branch + PR with both the blog post and the proxy fix.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **PostHog reverse proxy pattern**: PostHog recommends adding rewrites so browser requests go to `your-domain.com/ingest/*` → `us.i.posthog.com/*`. This bypasses ad-blockers that block `posthog.com` domains. The key endpoints are `/batch` (event ingestion), `/decide` (feature flags), and `/e` (legacy).
- **Vercel rewrites vs Next.js rewrites**: Both work, but `vercel.json` rewrites run at the edge (before Next.js), making them faster and more reliable for proxy use cases. Since `vercel.json` already exists with redirects, adding rewrites there keeps configuration centralized.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

content.content: The file /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/vercel.json has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Now I need to update the dashboard telemetry to use `/ingest` instead of the root domain, since PostHog's JS SDK needs the host pointed at the proxy path.

Wait — the dashboard runs on `localhost:7890`, not on `code-insights.app`. Let me re-read the dashboard setup to confirm.

The dashboard is served locally by Hono at `localhost:7890`. It sends telemetry to `https://code-insights.app` as the PostHog host. So the browser makes requests to `https://code-insights.app/batch` — which means the Vercel rewrite needs to handle `/batch` directly, not `/ingest/batch`.

Actually, PostHog's SDK sends to `{host}/batch`, so with `host: 'https://code-insights.app'` it hits `https://code-insights.app/batch`. Let me update the rewrites to match the actual paths PostHog uses.

content.content: The file /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/vercel.json has been updated successfully.
